summary: Tested GoHighLevel CRM integration end-to-end: admin-gated status/pipelines/test-contact routes, unauthenticated inbound webhook, live signup→GHL contact upsert, plus regression on escrow templates, email status, auth login, subscriptions/current. Verified all 5 sync hooks (signup, ceremony, escrow, HTS mint, subscription upgrade) are wired in their respective routes with asyncio.create_task + _safe() wrapper so GHL failures never break core flows. All 16 pytest tests pass (100% success).
critical_code_review_comments: ghl_service.py:115 — add_note() sends userId:null which GHL may reject for some accounts. Consider omitting userId entirely or reading from env.

ghl_routes.py _require_admin — inline token decode duplicates auth logic found elsewhere (e.g., deps/auth). Consider consolidating into a shared FastAPI dependency to reduce duplication across routes.
ghl_service.py module-level env reads (PIT_TOKEN/LOCATION_ID/etc.) happen at import time — any .env update requires supervisor restart. This is documented behavior but worth noting for ops.

Signup hook in auth_routes.py uses FastAPI BackgroundTasks while other hooks (escrow/HTS/subscription/ceremony) use asyncio.create_task. The two patterns are both valid but inconsistent — pick one for uniform operational semantics.
Failure safety for signup was verified via positive path only — could not mutate GHL_PIT_TOKEN at runtime from tests. _safe() wrapper inspection + code review confirms swallowing behavior.
seed_data_creation: Each run creates 2-3 live GHL contacts with email prefix 'ghltest_' in ClayTelligence sub-account. Prefix chosen so main agent can bulk-delete from GHL UI.
context_for_next_testing_agent: GHL integration is live against ClayTelligence (location 3SOWx6wvvlOLJ9h0bJfu, pipeline 0kIvOqYVlWs4KZWJgXW0). Use test_ghl_integration.py for regression. Signup hook verified via idempotent upsert (contacts/search has ~60s indexing delay, so don't use it). Escrow settlement → GHL sync and HTS mint → GHL sync hooks were code-reviewed and confirmed wired but not exercised end-to-end in this iteration (would require full escrow settle flow with biometric gate + HTS mint transaction). Code design (asyncio.create_task + _safe wrapper) guarantees CRM failures are non-fatal.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Initial 2 regression failures were test bugs (missing auth headers on /api/escrow/templates and /api/email/status which both require auth) — fixed in test file, all passing.
